title: 10 best herbal teas for oily skin
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oily skin are infusions made from plants that are particularly beneficial for reducing excess oil and improving the overall health of the skin. These teas contain various compounds, such as tannins, flavonoids, and antioxidants, that help regulate sebum production, reduce inflammation, and soothe irritation. Examples of herbal teas that are helpful for oily skin include green tea, chamomile, nettle, and yarrow. By incorporating these teas into their skincare routine, individuals can experience a decrease in shine, fewer breakouts, and an overall improvement in the appearance and texture of their skin. This can lead to increased confidence, a more balanced complexion, and a healthier relationship with one's skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent properties that help to balance the skin's natural oil production. The tea's calming and soothing effects also reduce inflammation, which can exacerbate acne and other skin issues caused by excess oil. Additionally, lemon balm contains antioxidants that combat free radicals, which can damage the skin and lead to premature aging. By drinking this tea regularly, individuals with oily skin can experience a reduction in breakouts and an improvement in overall skin clarity.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemon balm tea's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne, reducing the appearance of oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm tea help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and other skin irritations, allowing for a clearer complexion and reduced oil production.

Astringent properties: Lemon balm tea's astringent properties help to constrict the pores and balance the skin's natural oil production, reducing shine and excess oiliness without drying out the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and balance oil production in the skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant have natural astringent properties, making them effective at controlling sebum production and tightening pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from your garden or store dried leaves for later use.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves or three teaspoons of fresh leaves per 8 oz cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 195°f to 205°f in a tea kettle or pot.
Steep the lemon balm leaves in the hot water for five to seven minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, as desired, or store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent properties that help to balance the skin's pH levels, reducing excessive oil production. The menthol in peppermint also acts as a natural coolant, shrinking pores and tightening the skin, which can help to absorb excess sebum and reduce shine. Additionally, peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, leaving it feeling refreshed and balanced.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to clogged pores and excess oil production, thereby helping to clarify the skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness, irritation, and inflammation caused by oily skin, promoting a more even-toned complexion.

Astringent and antibacterial properties: The astringent and antibacterial compounds in peppermint tea can help tighten pores, reducing the appearance of large pores and acne-caused breakouts, while also combating bacterial growth that can exacerbate oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help to reduce inflammation and soothe acne-prone skin.
Bark: The bark of the peppermint plant contains tannins, which can help to reduce oil production and tighten pores, making it an effective treatment for oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store-dried peppermint leaves in airtight containers for freshness.
Measure two teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves or one tablespoon of fresh peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Bring filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan and let it simmer for five minutes to reduce the temperature.
Add the measured peppermint leaves to the simmering water and steep for seven to ten minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea into a teapot or individual cups using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with oily skin because it possesses natural antiseptic and astringent properties that help to regulate oil production and reduce pores. The antioxidants present in thyme also help to combat free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and breakouts associated with oily skin. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds found in thyme may help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation often caused by excessive oil secretion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme tea's antibacterial properties help to reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.
Astringent Properties: Thyme tea's astringent properties help to tighten pores and balance sebum production, which can help to control oiliness and prevent clogged pores.

Antioxidant Properties: Thyme tea's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily skin and premature aging.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are known for their astringent and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing oil production and soothing acne-prone skin.
Flowers: Thyme flowers have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and inflammation associated with oily skin, promoting a clearer complexion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thyme leaves from your garden or store-bought package measuring one teaspoon for every cup of water needed.
Combine the measured thyme with boiling water in a teapot or infuser allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids discarding any remaining thyme leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste adjusting sweetness and flavor as desired before serving hot or cold.
Store leftover tea in an airtight container refrigerated for up to 24 hours or frozen for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. The tea's flavonoids, such as camphor and salvene, help to reduce sebum production and tighten pores, leaving the skin looking clearer and less shiny. Additionally, sage's antifungal properties combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts. By incorporating sage tea into your skincare routine, you can say goodbye to greasy skin and hello to a more balanced complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Sage tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to oily skin conditions like acne and blackheads.

Astringent Properties: The astringent compounds present in sage tea help to tighten and constrict pores, reducing their size and allowing less oil to escape onto the surface of the skin, resulting in a more balanced complexion.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin conditions like acne and rosacea, promoting a clearer and more even-toned complexion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing acne caused by oily skin.
Bark: Sage bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce sebum production and soothe irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in sufficient quantity for desired dosage.
Measure one teaspoon of dried sage leaves or two tablespoons of fresh sage leaves per cup.
Combine the measured sage leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser, steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove solids.
Serve hot or at room temperature, sweetening with honey if desired and enjoying within a few hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent properties that help to reduce excess oil production, leaving skin feeling balanced and refreshed. The antiseptic qualities of rosemary also help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing breakouts. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties promote healthy skin cell growth, improving overall skin texture and tone, and reducing the appearance of pores.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate oily skin conditions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and rosacea, common issues in oily skin.

Antibacterial properties: Rosemary tea's antibacterial properties can help to combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that contributes to acne and oily skin, by reducing its growth and activity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary contain rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it effective in reducing oil production and acne.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves from your garden or store-bought package weighing approximately one tablespoon.
Combine the rosemary leaves with one quart of boiling water in a large pot or teapot allowing it to steep for ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large pitcher or individual cups discarding the solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste and adjust the temperature if desired serving the rosemary tea hot or iced.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days reheat as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram teas helps with oily skin because it possesses antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores. The tea's antioxidants also work to balance the skin's natural pH, regulating oil production and reducing sebum secretion. Additionally, marjoram's astringent qualities help to tighten pores and control excess oil, resulting in a clearer, more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Marjoram tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can contribute to oily skin by clogging pores and causing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants present in marjoram tea help to reduce inflammation and redness associated with oily skin, promoting a more balanced complexion.
Astringent properties: Marjoram tea's astringent nature helps to tighten and shrink the appearance of pores, reducing the visibility of blackheads and whiteheads.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to balance and regulate oil production in the skin, reducing acne and inflammation.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helps to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Flowers: A natural astringent that can help to tighten pores and reduce excess oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marjoram leaves from your garden or store-bought sources in quantities of one ounce for every eight ounces of water.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and pat dry with a paper towel if necessary.
Combine the marjoram leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot at a ratio of 1:8 for steeping times of five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids and preserving the liquid for consumption.
Serve the herbal marjoram tea hot or chilled with optional sweeteners like honey or sugar to mask any bitterness and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with oily skin because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that calms and soothes the skin. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and irritation, making it perfect for acne-prone skin. The tea's antibacterial agents help to eliminate bacteria that can clog pores, while its astringent qualities shrink pore size, reducing oil production and leaving skin looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: German Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can exacerbate oily skin conditions like acne.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in German Chamomile tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to oily skin.

Soothing and calming property: German Chamomile's soothing compounds, such as apigenin and chamazulene, calm the skin and reduce redness, which can help regulate sebum production and improve overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation associated with acne and oiliness.
Flowers: Known for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, the flowers are used to reduce redness, swelling, and acne lesions on oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried flowers from german chamomile plants in the morning for optimal potency.
Measure one teaspoon of dried flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured flowers with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the flowers from the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and consuming within 30 minutes of preparation for maximum benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with oily skin because its unique blend of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals helps to balance the body's natural detoxification processes. The tea's bitter compounds stimulate the liver to release toxins, which can contribute to acne and excess oil production on the skin. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties help to reduce water retention, further reducing puffiness and inflammation associated with oily skin. As a result, drinking dandelion teas can lead to clearer, more balanced skin.
remedy_properties: Laxative and diuretic properties: Dandelion tea helps to reduce oil production by stimulating bowel movements and increasing urine production, which can help to detoxify the body and remove excess toxins that can contribute to oily skin.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants in dandelion tea help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and cause inflammation, which can lead to acne, redness, and oiliness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin.

Choleretic and hepatoprotective properties: Dandelion tea's ability to stimulate bile production and protect the liver helps to improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can lead to a reduction in oiliness and acne-prone skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are a natural diuretic, helping to reduce excess oil production in the skin and promote detoxification.
Flowers: The flowers of the dandelion plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried dandelion flowers taraxacum officinale for every cup of tea you want to brew.
Combine one teaspoon of loose-leaf dandelion flowers with one cup of boiling water and let steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a teapot or cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste if desired and enjoy hot as an herbal remedy.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours for future consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with oily skin because it contains flavonoids and carotenoids that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce redness and soothe the skin, while also controlling sebum production. The tea's antiseptic qualities also aid in preventing bacterial growth, which can clog pores and exacerbate oily skin. Additionally, pot marigold teas has been shown to regulate the skin's natural barrier function, leading to a balanced and healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to oil production in the skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, rosacea, and other oily skin conditions, helping to prevent clogged pores and improve overall skin health.

Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tea's antimicrobial agents can help kill bacteria that thrive on oily skin, reducing the risk of infections and breakouts while promoting a healthy balance of skin flora.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling in acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oil production and acne.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers into a clean tea infuser or small muslin bag.
Heat fresh filtered water to boiling point in a large pot and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Pour the heated water over the dried calendula flowers and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or squeeze the muslin bag gently.
Enjoy the tea hot, adding honey or lemon to taste if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent properties that help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production. The tea's antibacterial compounds also work to combat acne-causing bacteria, preventing breakouts and reducing inflammation. Additionally, yarrow's antioxidant-rich composition helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily complexions. Regular consumption of this herbal tea can lead to a more balanced and healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Yarrow tea's antibacterial compounds help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin, soothing irritated pores and minimizing the appearance of acne scars.
Astringent properties: Yarrow tea's astringent compounds help to constrict pores, reducing excess oil production and tightening the skin to minimize the look of pores.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and saponins, which help to balance sebum production and reduce inflammation in the skin.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these delicate petals have astringent properties that help to tighten pores and reduce oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried yarrow flowers achillea millefolium leaves and stems for this recipe.
Measure one teaspoon of dried yarrow flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a tea infuser or strainer for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids after preparation is complete.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and consuming immediately.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily skin with herbal teas is by using them as toners. Brew strongly aromatic herbal teas like chamomile, green, or black tea, then let them cool. These teas contain tannins that help remove excess oil and reduce shine. After cleansing, apply the cooled tea to your face using a cotton ball. The astringent properties of these teas will help tighten pores and give your skin a matte finish. Regular use will help regulate oil production, leaving your skin looking fresher and healthier.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily skin that are treated with herbal teas include acne and skin inflammation. For acne, herbal teas like chamomile, spearmint, and green tea are often recommended due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These teas can help reduce redness, swelling, and the number of acne-causing bacteria on the skin. For skin inflammation, herbal teas such as calendula and echinacea can be beneficial. These teas have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and heal irritated skin. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can be helpful, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially for severe skin conditions.
